ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Kopi O Kosong (Singapore-style unsweetened black coffee). Mapped to the closest parent class 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301589). Kopi O Kosong is a traditional Singaporean kopitiam black coffee brewed from Robusta beans roasted with butter and sugar, served without added sugar or milk.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Coffee, black) used as the closest defensible match. Kopi O Kosong is a Singaporean variant of black coffee; no dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for this specific preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 33352005 (Coffee substance) is the closest available code. No specific SNOMED CT code exists for Kopi O Kosong as a prepared beverage.

main_ingredients: Robusta coffee beans (roasted with butter and sugar during roasting process)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of Kopi O Kosong as served at a Singapore kopitiam is approximately 300 g (ml), brewed black coffee with no added sugar or milk.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food — Kopi O (Black Coffee, Unsweetened)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Kopi O (black coffee, no sugar) used as primary source. Values for cholesterol, saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C are confirmed zero by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black coffee (FDC ID 171890), consistent with the HPB data. Potassium value sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171890.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Coffee, black, unsweetened
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kopi O Kosong specifically. GI of 29 is drawn from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for black coffee (unsweetened), which is the closest approved analogous food. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (29 × 1.2 g available carbohydrate per 300 g) / 100 = 0.35, rounded to 2 decimal places. Kopi O Kosong is functionally equivalent to unsweetened black coffee for GI purposes.

hypertension.key_factor: Zero sodium; moderate caffeine content from Robusta beans
hypertension.guidance: Kopi O Kosong contains zero sodium and no added sugar, posing no dietary sodium or sugar-driven blood pressure risk; however, individuals with hypertension should limit intake to no more than 2 cups per day as Robusta coffee is high in caffeine (~200 mg per 300 ml cup), which can acutely and transiently elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.4 g/100 g), no added sugar, low GI (29)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kopi O Kosong is essentially glycaemically neutral with only 0.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g, a GI of 29, and a glycemic load of 0.35 per standard cup; epidemiological evidence also associates regular unsweetened black coffee consumption with improved insulin sensitivity, making it suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes provided no sugar or condensed milk is added.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero trans fat, zero cholesterol; chlorogenic acid polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kopi O Kosong contains no saturated fat, trans fat, or cholesterol, and provides chlorogenic acids with documented antioxidant and endothelial-protective properties; moderate consumption of up to 3–4 cups per day has been associated with reduced cardiovascular disease risk in prospective cohort studies, though individuals with arrhythmias should monitor caffeine tolerance.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Polyphenol-rich (chlorogenic acids); zero pro-inflammatory fat or sugar
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Kopi O Kosong provides chlorogenic acids and other polyphenols with documented anti-inflammatory properties and contains zero sugar, fat, and trans fat, making it suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; limit to 2–3 cups per day as excessive caffeine may disrupt sleep and elevate cortisol, potentially worsening RA flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kopi O Kosong is a traditional Singaporean kopitiam beverage consisting of black coffee brewed from Robusta beans that have been roasted with butter and sugar during the roasting process, served hot without any added sugar or milk. The name derives from Hokkien: 'Kopi' means coffee, 'O' means black (without milk), and 'Kosong' means empty or zero (without sugar). It is a staple of Singapore's hawker centre and kopitiam culture, typically consumed at breakfast or as a mid-morning drink. The beverage has a bold, slightly bitter, and roasted flavour profile with a characteristic caramel-like aroma from the butter-roasted beans. Nutritionally, Kopi O Kosong is extremely low in calories (approximately 6 kcal per 300 ml cup), contains negligible carbohydrates (1.2 g per cup), zero fat, zero sodium, and zero cholesterol, making it one of the most diet-friendly hot beverages available. It is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. The chlorogenic acids and polyphenols present in coffee have been associated with improved insulin sensitivity, antioxidant activity, and anti-inflammatory effects. Caffeine content is relatively high due to the use of Robusta beans (~200 mg per 300 ml cup), which should be considered by caffeine-sensitive individuals. The beverage is vegan, dairy-free, gluten-free, and halal.
common_user_queries: Is Kopi O Kosong good for diabetics?
How many calories in Kopi O Kosong?
Can I drink Kopi O Kosong if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between Kopi O and Kopi O Kosong?
How much caffeine in Kopi O Kosong?

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient data sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database, which is the canonical approved source for Singapore dishes. The food is an exact match in the HPB database. Secondary micronutrient values (potassium) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171890, brewed coffee). GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for unsweetened black coffee. Confidence is high as the food identity, preparation, and nutritional profile are well-established.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171890) — black coffee contains no fat by its nature as an aqueous brew; fat_g is naturally zero. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for brewed black coffee; these are naturally zero as coffee is a fat-free aqueous beverage. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage. Vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — coffee contains negligible vitamin C after roasting; confirmed zero. Sodium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — naturally zero as no salt is added. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed micronutrient data for Kopi O Kosong as prepared in Singapore kopitiam style are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this entry, and the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy match for this specific Robusta-based preparation is insufficient to assign values with high confidence: vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Sources consulted that confirmed unavailability for this specific preparation: HPB Singapore Foodo (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/, accessed 2026-04-16) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171890 (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/, accessed 2026-04-16). The original input record cited GrabFood as the source for cholesterol, saturated_fat, trans_fat, and vitamin_c; GrabFood is a forbidden source under Project Veritas v2 source policy and has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy values, which are consistent. Potassium_mg value of 49.0 mg/100 g is sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171890, brewed coffee) as HPB does not report potassium for this item. GI of 29 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for unsweetened black coffee; no direct GI measurement for Kopi O Kosong exists in the approved GI literature. The input glycemic_index value of 55 (medium) has been corrected to 29 (low) based on the approved University of Sydney GI Database source for black coffee.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (0.0), saturated_fat_g (0.0), sugar_g (0.4) all present as numeric values, plausible and supported by HPB and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (29), gi_category ('low'), glycemic_load_per_serving (0.35), and gi_source all present. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for unsweetened black coffee with estimation basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI (FOODON_03301589), FoodEx2 code (A00LC), and SNOMED CT code (33352005) all present and defensible. Gap notes provided for all three explaining closest-parent match rationale.
